message:
fix(freeze): handle Infinity in NaN-boxing scheme

Fix isFloat() and toJSValuePtr() to properly recognize +/- Infinity.

The NaN-boxing scheme uses the QNaN space (exponent 0x7FF with quiet
bit set) for tagging non-float values. However, Infinity also has
exponent 0x7FF but with mantissa = 0.

Previously, Infinity was incorrectly classified as a NaN-boxed tag
and converted to undefined when passed to native functions like
console.log.

The fix checks: if exponent is 0x7FF and mantissa is 0, it's Infinity
(not a tagged value). This fixes _.flattenDeep and other lodash
functions that use Infinity as a depth parameter.
